name: Loop 2: The Péguère wall – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 2 : Le mur de Péguère — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This loop of almost 73 kilometers from Foix is a real challenge. The wall of Péguère (the nickname given to the climb of the pass of the same name) fully deserves this nickname. Scheduled during the 1973 Tour de France, its ascent was refused because the descent was deemed too dangerous. It was not until 2012, 39 years later, that the organizers of the Tour de France decided to go there again.

You take the Devil's Bridge at the eighth kilometer, then follow the course of the Ariège to Tarascon. The small departmental road 618 runs parallel to the national road 20 and thus avoids car traffic.

Once at kilometer 20, the serious things begin, first gradually passing through the high line, then after 40 kilometers, you arrive at the foot of the wall. A few very trying kilometers, and here you are at the top.

At the pass, a pastoral shelter and an orientation table, allow you to catch your breath and your mind.

The beautiful descent that follows takes you through the Col de Jouels. It is singular enough to be noted. It's not every day that you cross a pass in this way.

Back in Foix, take the opportunity to visit its castle. This jewel, which dates from the 12th century, stands on a rocky platform and dominates the city. It is the perfect example of what a stately home was like in the Middle Ages. A very beautiful exhibition on armory at that time awaits you within its walls.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Foix are situated in the heart of the Ariège Pyrenees, offering a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region features towering Pyrenean mountains, deep valleys carved by the Ariège River, and extensive forests of oak and chestnut. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from challenging mountain passes with significant elevation gains to more moderate routes through rolling hills and along river plains. This geographical composition provides a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Foix The most popular road cycling route is Loop 2: The Péguère wall – 10 Pyrenean routes, a difficult 45.2 miles (72.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 4 minutes to complete. This route features the challenging "Péguère wall" with steep gradients and significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Loop 1: The four passes – 10 Pyrenean itineraries, a difficult 63.6 miles (102.3 km) path. This route traverses multiple Pyrenean passes, offering extensive climbs and panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Pailhères Pass – emblematic passes of the Pyrenees, a 66.4 miles (106.9 km) trail leading through high Pyrenean terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 37 minutes. Road cycling around Foix is defined by Pyrenean mountains, the Ariège Valley, and dense forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging ascents to more moderate rides. The routes in Foix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 18,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Foix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Foix?
A: There are over 300 road cycling routes in and around Foix, catering to various skill levels. These include challenging mountain passes and more moderate rides through valleys and forests.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Foix?
A: Yes, while Foix is known for challenging climbs, there are also easier options. The Vélosud (V81) cycle route offers a relatively flat path along the Ariège River, connecting Foix to Mirepoix using greenways and quiet minor roads. For more moderate rides, consider routes that traverse rolling hills rather than steep mountain passes.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling routes in the Foix region?
A: The Foix region is famous for its formidable Pyrenean passes. Some of the most challenging routes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206163376/loop-2-the-peguere-wall-10-pyrenean-routes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop 2: The Péguère wall&lt;/a&gt;, known for its steep 16% gradients, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068532124/pailheres-pass-emblematic-passes-of-the-pyrenees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pailhères Pass&lt;/a&gt;, an emblematic Pyrenean climb reaching over 2,000 meters. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206162084/loop-1-the-four-passes-10-pyrenean-itineraries" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop 1: The four passes&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Foix?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945206935/col-de-peguere-loop-from-foix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Péguère Loop from Foix&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e935118641/prat-d-albis-loop-from-foix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prat d'Albis Loop from Foix&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that allow you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling around Foix?
A: The routes around Foix offer diverse and stunning landscapes. You'll cycle through towering Pyrenean mountains, deep valleys carved by the Ariège River, and extensive forests of oak and chestnut. Expect panoramic views from high passes, lush green mountain pastures, and the pastoral ambiance of plateaus like Prat d'Albis.

Q: What are some notable attractions or viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter several points of interest. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lers Pass&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3778020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tristaina Solar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent spot for panoramic vistas. The town of Foix itself is dominated by the impressive Château de Foix, visible from many surrounding routes.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Foix?
A: June and July are particularly popular months for road cycling in the Foix region, offering generally favorable weather conditions for tackling both mountain passes and valley routes. However, the season typically extends from late spring through early autumn.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes or multi-day tours in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers options for longer rides. The 'Cyclotour de l'Ariège in 5 days' is a difficult multi-day option that incorporates several legendary Pyrenean passes. For a less strenuous long-distance option, the Vélosud (V81) provides a continuous route along the Ariège River, which can be part of a longer journey. More information on the Vélosud can be found on &lt;a href="https://en.francevelotourisme.com/cycle-route/the-velosud/foix-mirepoix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;France Vélo Tourisme&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Foix?
A: The road cycling routes around Foix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging ascents, the stunning Pyrenean scenery, and the variety of routes available for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there routes that explore the Ariège Valley?
A: Yes, the Ariège Valley is a prominent feature of the region, carved by the Ariège River. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206174846/loop-6-the-beille-plateau-and-ariege-valley-10-pyrenean-routes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop 6: The Beille plateau and Ariège valley&lt;/a&gt; specifically incorporate this scenic area, offering views of the river and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that include the Prat d'Albis plateau?
A: Absolutely. The vast Prat d'Albis plateau, high above Foix, is a popular spot. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e935118641/prat-d-albis-loop-from-foix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prat d'Albis Loop from Foix&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated route that takes you through this area, known for its pastoral ambiance and grazing cows in summer.
tips:
This stone bridge allows you to cross the Ariège and drive on small roads that overlook the river. This way you avoid traffic between Foix and Tarascon-sur-Ariège.

The construction of this bridge is linked to a legend whose protagonist is the devil.

Located downstream of the Ariège in relation to the town of Foix, this very pretty town is the ideal base camp for many excursions in the surrounding mountains. The upper town was once fortified, and you can still make out the Castella tower which overlooks Tarascon.

Every year, in May, the big Fair takes place in the town where many specific animals of the region such as Merens horses and Gascon cows are presented to the public.

These two endemic species are the pride of breeders.

After four difficult kilometers, this last stretch sees the average gradient increase and the views become "out of this world".

It's a really great stretch of road.

With 18% in the climb in places, the road to the Col de Péguère lives up to its nickname of wall. It's a real challenge, even for experienced cyclists. In the end, the reward is there: splendid views of the mountains.
A very beautiful little town with a certain charm. If you're in the area, you should definitely stop by.
